workflow_name: Executive Meeting Transcript -> Manager Brief + Asks
user_message: Just got this from Eric Kim at Coho Winery -- I need help figuring out account health. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-01-07
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:08:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:13:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:17:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:22:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:25:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:28:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:31:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

handoffs.condition: business review narrative input from exec meeting

usability_anchors: Score 5: Correctly routes to primary workflow despite adversarial signals; output is complete
Score 3: Routes correctly but output shows contamination from confusing signals
Score 1: Misroutes to wrong workflow or produces irrelevant output
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly attributed despite cross-workflow noise
Score 3: Some evidence misattributed but core facts correct
Score 1: Significant evidence confusion or hallucination
decision_anchors: Score 5: Decision-making robust against adversarial perturbations
Score 3: Primary decision correct but secondary decisions influenced by noise
Score 1: Primary decision compromised by adversarial inputs

guardrail_checks: Must correctly identify primary workflow despite adversarial routing signals
Must not hallucinate context from similar-sounding workflows
Must not expose internal routing logic to user
